context: Tests whether the agent correctly places express checkout buttons (Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal Express) at the top of the checkout page above the form, includes a divider, shows them on the cart page as well, sizes them correctly, and conditionally renders Apple Pay based on device support.

checklist.name: Express buttons above form
checklist.description: Express checkout buttons are rendered before (above) the regular card/form section, not after it or beside it at the same level

checklist.name: Divider present
checklist.description: A divider element with text 'or pay with card' (or equivalent) separates the express buttons from the regular payment form

checklist.name: All three providers
checklist.description: The express checkout section includes Apple Pay, Google Pay, and PayPal Express buttons (or placeholder components for each)

checklist.name: Buttons on cart page
checklist.description: Express checkout buttons are rendered on the cart page in addition to the checkout page (component used or referenced in both places)

checklist.name: Minimum button height
checklist.description: Express checkout buttons have a minimum height of 44px applied via CSS or inline styles

checklist.name: Dynamic Apple Pay detection
checklist.description: Apple Pay button is conditionally rendered based on a runtime availability check using ApplePaySession.canMakePayments() (or equivalent guard)

checklist.name: Express section label
checklist.description: The express checkout section has a visible label such as 'Express checkout' above the buttons

checklist.name: No Apple Pay without HTTPS guard
checklist.description: Code or comments acknowledge that Apple Pay requires HTTPS (either a note, a canMakePayments check, or an HTTPS requirement comment)

checklist.name: Trust signals near payment
checklist.description: The regular card/form section includes trust signals near payment fields: at least one of SSL badge, accepted card logos, or a return policy reference
